signals.headline: Bank of Korea Holds Policy Rate at 2
signals.raw:
Bank of Korea Holds Policy Rate at 2.5% on April 10, Noting Higher-Than-Expected Inflation Trajectories | NO | HIGH confidence** [2][5]

March 2026 annual inflation rose to 2.2% YoY, up from 2.0% in January-February but below 2.4% forecasts, driven by energy costs amid global tensions despite subsidies.[1][2] The BoK's April 10 decision emphasized data-dependent policy amid rising price pressures
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?
